column1: A team created a file named sampleTests.py containing multiple test functions starting with test_. When running pytest
What is the most likely reason?
B) File name does not match pytest discovery pattern CORRECT
C) Functions must start with run_
D) pytest does not support standalone functions
A test should run only on Linux OS. On Windows
A QA engineer wants to skip all tests in a file temporarily due to a known bug.
You have 20 test methods in a class. A database connection should open once before all tests and close after all tests.
A login test should validate 5 username/password combinations.
Which is the most efficient solution?
B) Use for loop inside test
How many test cases will run?
A test fails due to a known bug. The team wants it to run but not fail the test suite.
Parent class has setup_method. Child class inherits it.
Will setup execute for child test methods?
Case Study 1: Skipping Based on API Version
Your API test should run only if version >= 2.0. Otherwise skip automatically.
Some tests take 10 minutes. The team wants to exclude them during quick regression runs.
B) Add custom marker like @pytest.mark.slow CORRECT
Every test needs login first. Current approach duplicates login code in 50 tests.
B) Method name does not start with test_ CORRECT
A test fails before completion. Team notices teardown_method is still executed.
A) teardown runs only on success
B) teardown runs regardless of test result CORRECT
Case Study 6: Parametrize with Multiple Arguments
Will pytest discover it by default?
When should xfail be preferred over skip?
B) When known bug exists but test should still run CORRECT
